Title: Senior Data Systems Analyst
Description:
ZoomInfo is where careers accelerate. We move fast, think boldly, and empower you to do the best work of your life. You’ll be surrounded by teammates who care deeply, challenge each other, and celebrate wins. With tools that amplify your impact and a culture that backs your ambition, you won’t just contribute. You’ll make things happen–fast.

ZoomInfo is seeking a Senior Data Analyst to become the expert on our business entity data pipeline—the system that ingests, processes, and profiles millions of company records that power our customers' go-to-market strategies.

In this role, you'll build deep expertise in how our business entity data flows from acquisition through profiling and output. You'll understand how data is transformed and processed through the pipeline, bring informed opinions to technical conversations, and help shape the evolution of our data quality capabilities. As you build mastery of our systems, you'll increasingly lead strategic data improvement initiatives that require both systems thinking and creative problem-solving.

This isn't about building dashboards or SQL reports. This is about understanding how data behaves as it moves through systems, solving complex data quality challenges, and ensuring our pipeline continuously evolves to meet customer needs and maintain competitive advantage.

Who You Are

Systems Thinker with Technical Depth: You understand how data systems work, not just what they produce. You've worked with data pipelines or ETL workflows and can review code (Python, Java, SQL) to understand data transformations and trace how data flows through systems.

B2B Data & GTM Domain Expert: You understand firmographic data and how sales and marketing teams use company attributes (employee counts, locations, hierarchies, technology usage, industry classifications) for targeting and segmentation. You've worked with B2B sales data or revenue operations and can evaluate data quality through both a technical and business lens.

Opinionated Technical Contributor: You have informed opinions on how things should work. You assess technical tradeoffs, evaluate solution feasibility, and contribute meaningfully to conversations with engineers based on your understanding.

Growth-Oriented Problem Solver: You're excited to build deep expertise in a complex domain and take on strategic projects. You've tackled ambiguous problems that required figuring things out as you went.

Analytical and Hands-On: You're equally comfortable writing code to analyze data patterns and manually investigating edge cases. You dig into details when needed and know when to zoom out to see the bigger picture.

What You'll Do

Build Data Quality Expertise


Master how our business entity data behaves as it flows through our pipeline: what transforms are applied at each stage, where quality issues emerge, and how changes impact downstream data
Review production code to understand how data is processed and assess how proposed changes would impact data quality
Create documentation and system maps that capture how data is processed, dependencies, and quality considerations


Drive Pipeline Improvements


Partner with Engineering and Product on pipeline enhancements, bringing data quality insights, business impact assessments, and informed opinions on data requirements
Validate improvements through rigorous testing and hands-on verification of data quality
Translate data quality investigations into system-level improvement opportunities


Solve Complex Data Challenges


Lead data improvement initiatives that require systems thinking and creative problem-solving (improving location verification, integrating new data sources, or solving novel extraction challenges)
Tackle ambiguous problems through understanding processing logic, manual investigation, and cross-functional coordination
Build repeatable approaches to testing, validation, and root cause analysis


Required

What You'll Bring


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or related quantitative field
5+ years of experience in data analytics or related technical roles with focus on data quality and pipeline systems
Experience working with data pipelines, ETL systems, or data processing workflows where you understand how data moves through systems and what can go wrong
Strong skills investigating data quality issues through profiling data, identifying anomalies, tracing root causes, and validating fixes
Ability to understand data transformations by reviewing SQL, Python, Java, or similar code
Strong programming skills in Python and SQL for data analysis, profiling, and manipulation
Experience solving ambiguous, multi-faceted data problems that required figuring out the approach, not just executing well-defined analyses
Demonstrated ability to work effectively with Engineering and/or Product teams, translating between technical implementation and business needs
Self-directed with strong ownership mentality; you drive your work forward and know when to seek input


Strongly Preferred


Ability to work hybrid from our Vancouver, WA or Waltham, MA office locations
Experience working with firmographic/company data in B2B context
Experience with data profiling, entity resolution, record linkage, or data matching systems
Background in web data acquisition, data extraction, or data collection systems
Knowledge of web data structures (HTML/DOM) and data extraction methodologies
Experience designing validation strategies or testing frameworks for data quality
Experience creating technical documentation or knowledge-sharing resources
Track record of leading or co-leading cross-functional data projects


This Role Is Not a Fit If

You're looking to focus primarily on building dashboards, creating reports, or analyzing business metrics. While you'll do analysis in this role, the core mission is understanding and improving how business entity data is processed and profiled through our pipeline systems.

This Role Is a Strong Fit If

You want to become the expert on how business entity data flows through systems and drive strategic initiatives. You're excited to develop deep technical expertise in data quality, contribute informed opinions to data requirements, and progressively take on more ownership as you master the domain. You enjoy solving problems where the answer isn't obvious and seeing your work directly improve product capabilities and customer success.

Actual compensation offered will be based on factors such as the candidate’s work location, qualifications, skills, experience and/or training. Your recruiter can share more information about the specific salary range for your desired work location during the hiring process. We want our employees and their families to thrive.

In addition to comprehensive benefits we offer holistic mind, body and lifestyle programs designed for overall well-being. Learn more about ZoomInfo benefits here.

Below is the US base salary for this position. Additional compensation such as Bonus, Commission, Equity and other benefits may also apply.

$102,200—$160,600 USD

About Us

ZoomInfo (NASDAQ: GTM) is the Go-To-Market Intelligence Platform that empowers businesses to grow faster with AI-ready insights, trusted data, and advanced automation. Its solutions provide more than 35,000 companies worldwide with a complete view of their customers, making every seller their best seller.

ZoomInfo is committed to protecting your privacy when you apply for jobs with us. Please review our Job Applicant Privacy Notice for more details on how we handle your personal information.

ZoomInfo may use a software-based assessment as part of the recruitment process. More information about this tool, including the results of the most recent bias audit, is available here.

ZoomInfo is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, hiring based on qualifications, merit, and business needs, and does not discriminate based on protected status. We welcome all applicants and are committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of sex, race, age, color, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability status, religion, protected military or veteran status, medical condition, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We also consider qualified candidates with criminal histories in accordance with legal requirements.

For Massachusetts Applicants: It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. ZoomInfo does not administer lie detector tests to applicants in any location.

Title: Cost Analyst
Description:
Why choose between doing meaningful work and having a fulfilling life? At MITRE, you can have both. That's because MITRE people are committed to tackling our nation's toughest challenges—and we're committed to the long-term well-being of our employees. MITRE is different from most technology companies. We are a not-for-profit corporation chartered to work for the public interest, with no commercial conflicts to influence what we do. The R&D centers we operate for the government create lasting impact in fields as diverse as cybersecurity, healthcare, aviation, defense, and enterprise transformation. We're making a difference every day—working for a safer, healthier, and more secure nation and world. Our workplace reflects our values. We offer competitive benefits, exceptional professional development opportunities for career growth, and a culture of innovation that embraces adaptability, collaboration, technical excellence, and people in partnership. If this sounds like the choice you want to make, then choose MITRE - and make a difference with us.

Department Summary:

We are seeking an associate cost analyst to join our team. The MITRE Corporation’s Business Analytics and Acquisition Affordability Department is seeking a cost analyst to support multiple projects solving our sponsors’ most critical economic and financial challenges. This department serves as a vanguard for MITRE to meet the future needs of our sponsors requiring technical excellence while encouraging creativity and fostering interest in the intersection of emerging technologies, cost, business, and economic impacts across its diverse membership. Expect a multi-disciplinary team environment with self-motivated, highly trained professionals. The work includes engaging with sponsors to scope and prioritize tasks, collecting large datasets to perform data-driven analyses, conducting market research, investigating relevant policies and regulations, understanding the current budgetary and political environment, and performing additional research and analysis as necessary. This is a project-oriented position that may require project management, collaboration, communicating to technical and non-technical audiences, and direct sponsor engagement.

The Business Analytics and Acquisition Affordability Department within the Acquisition Division in MITRE Technology and Engineering solves our sponsor’s problems by leveraging our capabilities within:


Cost Estimation and Analysis
Investment Economics & Decision Analysis
Strategic Business & Financial Analytics


This position supports the department’s capabilities in cost, economic, business analyses, and data science as needed. That work could include life-cycle cost estimating, tradeoff and affordability analyses, decision analysis, portfolio investment analysis, economic analysis and modeling, research, business case analysis, modeling and simulation, advanced statistical techniques, and constrained optimization.


Expect a dynamic team environment where tasks are rarely routine and often require novel approaches.
Expect to work on multiple projects simultaneously, with limited structure and supervision.


Candidates should expect a dynamic and collaborative environment where tasks are rarely routine and often require innovative and adaptive approaches. Successful team members are comfortable working across multiple projects simultaneously, operating with limited structure or supervision, and engaging directly with sponsors to solve complex, ambiguous problems.

Roles & Responsibilities:


Key functions include developing models and performing qualitative and quantitative analyses to understand the cost/economic/financial impact of investments, risk mitigation strategies, options and other government actions
Collecting and analyzing financial, cost, and economic data
Develop and/or evaluate program technical and programmatic baselines
Develop innovative development, production and operating and support (O&S) cost estimating techniques for a broad range of cost elements
Develop independent or program life cycle cost estimates (LCCEs) and accompanying cost and schedule risk, uncertainty, and sensitivity analysis
Develop cost estimates in support of source selection, including Independent Government Cost Estimates (IGCEs)
Perform cost-related studies and analysis, e.g,, on rate reasonableness
Develop investment economic and decision analyses, including business case analyses, cost/benefit analyses, analysis-of- alternatives, and portfolio analysis
Develop strategic business and financial analyses, including but not limited to economic studies, cost recovery strategies, budget development & planning, and affordability analysis
Assess the credibility of government and industry cost estimates using benchmark guidance, such as the GAO 12 steps and the Cost Estimating Body of Knowledge (CEBoK)
Assess the credibility of government and industry cost savings initiatives data.
Conducting surveys and utilizing various sampling techniques.
Researching various fields including politics, national security, healthcare, education, energy, etc.
Making use of historical data and analyzing market trends.
Assist in identifying sources of information and gathering a wide range of quantitative and qualitative data to support decisions that have implications for MITRE and its government sponsors
Apply analysis skills, including exploratory data analysis, time series analysis, and linear regression
Draw conclusions from analyses and clearly and concisely articulate key findings in both written and verbal form
Assist in developing data visualization products that improve the presentation of complex data sets
Translate unstructured data and nontraditional sources of information into business insights
Performing data cleaning, visualization and analysis in standard statistical and graphics software languages such as R, Stata and Excel


Basic Qualifications:


Bachelor of Arts/Science degree in one of the fields (or related) listed below, and a minimum of 2 years of related experience; or an advanced degree with relevant experience who can immediately contribute at this job step; or equivalent combination of related education and work experience.
Economics
Engineering
Mathematics
Statistics
Operations Research
Data Science/Analytics
Quantitative Sciences
Proficient at use of statistical methods including regressions, forecasting, and hypothesis testing
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Proficient with MS Office Suite, especially Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
Ability to obtain and maintain a Top Secret level security clearance
Per the U.S. Government’s eligibility requirements, you must be a U.S Citizen to be considered for a security clearance.
This position requires a minimum of 4 days a week on-site


Preferred Qualifications:


Familiarity with software engineering best practices and agile devops software development processes
Experience with econometric/economic modeling and analysis
Working knowledge of federal budgeting and spending mechanics, processes, and data.
Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE) and OMB Circular A-11
Acquisition reform and PPBE reform initiatives
Federal Acquisition Regulations
Capital Planning and Investment Control (CPIC)
Federal spending data, including: USASpending.gov, SAM.gov, or FPDS-NG
Investment modeling and analysis, cost benefit analysis, experience in handling large datasets for data driven analysis, policy and investment analysis, and economic analysis in support of regulatory policy, pre- and post-implementation analysis, forecasting, and applied research
Source selection cost evaluation experience
Expertise in using parametric cost estimating tools (PRICE, SEER SEM, etc.)
ICEAA Certification or Defense Acquisition Workforce Initiative Act (DAWIA) certification desired
Knowledge and understanding of federal government budget and spending rules, processes, and procedures
Demonstrated interest in public service and advancing mission-focused outcomes.

HTML:
Why choose between doing meaningful work and having a fulfilling life? At MITRE, you can have both. That's because MITRE people are committed to tackling our nation's toughest challenges—and we're committed to the long-term well-being of our employees. MITRE is different from most technology companies. We are a not-for-profit corporation chartered to work for the public interest, with no commercial conflicts to influence what we do. The R&D centers we operate for the government create lasting impact in fields as diverse as cybersecurity, healthcare, aviation, defense, and enterprise transformation. We're making a difference every day—working for a safer, healthier, and more secure nation and world. Our workplace reflects our values. We offer competitive benefits, exceptional professional development opportunities for career growth, and a culture of innovation that embraces adaptability, collaboration, technical excellence, and people in partnership. If this sounds like the choice you want to make, then choose MITRE - and make a difference with us.

Department Summary:

We are seeking an associate cost analyst to join our team. The MITRE Corporation’s Business Analytics and Acquisition Affordability Department is seeking a cost analyst to support multiple projects solving our sponsors’ most critical economic and financial challenges. This department serves as a vanguard for MITRE to meet the future needs of our sponsors requiring technical excellence while encouraging creativity and fostering interest in the intersection of emerging technologies, cost, business, and economic impacts across its diverse membership. Expect a multi-disciplinary team environment with self-motivated, highly trained professionals. The work includes engaging with sponsors to scope and prioritize tasks, collecting large datasets to perform data-driven analyses, conducting market research, investigating relevant policies and regulations, understanding the current budgetary and political environment, and performing additional research and analysis as necessary. This is a project-oriented position that may require project management, collaboration, communicating to technical and non-technical audiences, and direct sponsor engagement.

The Business Analytics and Acquisition Affordability Department within the Acquisition Division in MITRE Technology and Engineering solves our sponsor’s problems by leveraging our capabilities within:

  • Cost Estimation and Analysis
  • Investment Economics & Decision Analysis
  • Strategic Business & Financial Analytics

This position supports the department’s capabilities in cost, economic, business analyses, and data science as needed. That work could include life-cycle cost estimating, tradeoff and affordability analyses, decision analysis, portfolio investment analysis, economic analysis and modeling, research, business case analysis, modeling and simulation, advanced statistical techniques, and constrained optimization.

  • Expect a dynamic team environment where tasks are rarely routine and often require novel approaches.
  • Expect to work on multiple projects simultaneously, with limited structure and supervision.

Candidates should expect a dynamic and collaborative environment where tasks are rarely routine and often require innovative and adaptive approaches. Successful team members are comfortable working across multiple projects simultaneously, operating with limited structure or supervision, and engaging directly with sponsors to solve complex, ambiguous problems.

Roles & Responsibilities:

  • Key functions include developing models and performing qualitative and quantitative analyses to understand the cost/economic/financial impact of investments, risk mitigation strategies, options and other government actions
  • Collecting and analyzing financial, cost, and economic data
  • Develop and/or evaluate program technical and programmatic baselines
  • Develop innovative development, production and operating and support (O&S) cost estimating techniques for a broad range of cost elements
  • Develop independent or program life cycle cost estimates (LCCEs) and accompanying cost and schedule risk, uncertainty, and sensitivity analysis
  • Develop cost estimates in support of source selection, including Independent Government Cost Estimates (IGCEs)
  • Perform cost-related studies and analysis, e.g,, on rate reasonableness
  • Develop investment economic and decision analyses, including business case analyses, cost/benefit analyses, analysis-of- alternatives, and portfolio analysis
  • Develop strategic business and financial analyses, including but not limited to economic studies, cost recovery strategies, budget development & planning, and affordability analysis
  • Assess the credibility of government and industry cost estimates using benchmark guidance, such as the GAO 12 steps and the Cost Estimating Body of Knowledge (CEBoK)
  • Assess the credibility of government and industry cost savings initiatives data.
  • Conducting surveys and utilizing various sampling techniques.
  • Researching various fields including politics, national security, healthcare, education, energy, etc.
  • Making use of historical data and analyzing market trends.
  • Assist in identifying sources of information and gathering a wide range of quantitative and qualitative data to support decisions that have implications for MITRE and its government sponsors
  • Apply analysis skills, including exploratory data analysis, time series analysis, and linear regression
  • Draw conclusions from analyses and clearly and concisely articulate key findings in both written and verbal form
  • Assist in developing data visualization products that improve the presentation of complex data sets
  • Translate unstructured data and nontraditional sources of information into business insights
  • Performing data cleaning, visualization and analysis in standard statistical and graphics software languages such as R, Stata and Excel

Basic Qualifications:

  • Bachelor of Arts/Science degree in one of the fields (or related) listed below, and a minimum of 2 years of related experience; or an advanced degree with relevant experience who can immediately contribute at this job step; or equivalent combination of related education and work experience.
    • Economics
    • Engineering
    • Mathematics
    • Statistics
    • Operations Research
    • Data Science/Analytics
    • Quantitative Sciences
  • Proficient at use of statistical methods including regressions, forecasting, and hypothesis testing
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Proficient with MS Office Suite, especially Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
  • Ability to obtain and maintain a Top Secret level security clearance
  • Per the U.S. Government’s eligibility requirements, you must be a U.S Citizen to be considered for a security clearance.
  • This position requires a minimum of 4 days a week on-site

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Familiarity with software engineering best practices and agile devops software development processes
  • Experience with econometric/economic modeling and analysis
  • Working knowledge of federal budgeting and spending mechanics, processes, and data.
    • Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE) and OMB Circular A-11
    • Acquisition reform and PPBE reform initiatives
    • Federal Acquisition Regulations
    • Capital Planning and Investment Control (CPIC)
    • Federal spending data, including: USASpending.gov, SAM.gov, or FPDS-NG
  • Investment modeling and analysis, cost benefit analysis, experience in handling large datasets for data driven analysis, policy and investment analysis, and economic analysis in support of regulatory policy, pre- and post-implementation analysis, forecasting, and applied research
  • Source selection cost evaluation experience
  • Expertise in using parametric cost estimating tools (PRICE, SEER SEM, etc.)
  • ICEAA Certification or Defense Acquisition Workforce Initiative Act (DAWIA) certification desired
  • Knowledge and understanding of federal government budget and spending rules, processes, and procedures
  • Demonstrated interest in public service and advancing mission-focused outcomes.
  • Strong foundation in Python and SQL and familiarity with modern data processing tools or frameworks (e.g., Pandas, Spark, Databricks, Airflow).

None

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Apply now »

Date: Jun 10, 2026

Location: San Jose, California, United States

Company: Super Micro Computer

Job Req ID: 29246

About Supermicro

Supermicro® is a Top Tier provider of advanced server, storage, and networking solutions for Data Center, Cloud Computing, Enterprise IT, Hadoop/ Big Data, Hyperscale, HPC and IoT/Embedded customers worldwide. We are the #5 fastest growing company among the Silicon Valley Top 50 technology firms. Our unprecedented global expansion has provided us with the opportunity to offer a large number of new positions to the technology community. We seek talented, passionate, and committed engineers, technologists, and business leaders to join us.

Job Summary

Supermicro is seeking a Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will work closely with Sales, Operations, Product Management, and Procurement teams to coordinate forecast meetings, foster cross-team alignment, and support planning initiatives.

Strong communication skills, multitasking ability, and deadline management are essential. You will generate reports, analyze trends, and interpret data using tools such as Excel, SQL, and BI dashboards, ensuring data accuracy and thorough validation.

Essential Duties And Responsibilities


Prepare and deliver monthly reports to support forecasting and business review processes.
Assist in developing and enhancing data management systems to improve forecasting capabilities.
Maintain and update forecasts on a daily and weekly basis to reflect the latest trends and insights.
Work closely with cross-functional teams—including Sales, Project Management, Product Management, and Procurement—to facilitate and support Sales Forecast meetings, ensuring alignment and effective collaboration.
Support system enhancements and changes related to operations and data workflows.
Provide strategic and analytical support to management for organizational planning and decision-making.
Ensure data accuracy and integrity by applying best practices in data collection, validation, and analysis.
Monitor internal client and team feedback to identify and recommend process improvements.
Champion and uphold the company’s mission, vision, and core values in daily activities.
Perform additional duties and responsibilities as assigned by management.


Qualifications


SQL Server proficiency and Data modeling — including complex queries, joins, CTEs, window functions, stored procedures, performance tuning, database design, normalization, and schema interpretation.
Microsoft 365 analytics tools: Excel, Power BI and SharePoint/OneDrive.
Data quality and validation — ability to identify anomalies, inconsistencies, and integrity issues.
Basic scripting — Python or R for data manipulation or automation.
Windows environment fluency — file systems, permissions, scheduled tasks, and general troubleshooting.
Analytical & Business Skills — Skilled in translating business questions into measurable insights through KPI development, dashboard reporting, data analysis, and adherence to data governance, security, and compliance best practices.


Required Experience


2–5 years of experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or reporting role.
Hands‑on experience working with Microsoft SQL Server in production environments.
Experience building and maintaining Power BI dashboards or similar BI tools.
Demonstrated history of supporting business teams (finance, operations, sales, HR, etc.) with data insights.
Experience working with large datasets, data cleansing, and data validation.
Prior work in environments using Microsoft 365 for collaboration and reporting.


Preferred / Nice‑to‑Have


Experience with SSRS (SQL Server Reporting Services).
Familiarity with version control (Git) for SQL scripts or analytics code.
Experience with statistical analysis or forecasting.


Salary Range

$90,000 - $110,000

The salary offered will depend on several factors, including your location, level, education, training, specific skills, years of experience, and comparison to other employees already in this role. In addition to a comprehensive benefits package, candidates may be eligible for other forms of compensation, such as participation in bonus and equity award programs.

EEO Statement

Supermicro is an Equal Opportunity Employer and embraces diversity in our employee population. It is the policy of Supermicro to provide equal opportunity to all qualified applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, protected veteran status or special disabled veteran, marital status, pregnancy, genetic information, or any other legally protected status.

Job Segment: Cloud, Database, SQL, Developer, Sharepoint, Technology

Title: Data Analyst II
Description:
Job Description

Data Analyst II

We are Lennar

Lennar is one of the nation's leading homebuilders, dedicated to making an impact and creating an extraordinary experience for their Homeowners, Communities, and Associates by building quality homes and providing exceptional customer service, giving back to the communities in which we work and live in, and fostering a culture of opportunity and growth for our Associates throughout their career. Lennar has been recognized as a Fortune 500® company and consistently ranked among the top homebuilders in the United States.

Join a Company that Empowers you to Build your Future

A Data Analyst II is a developing professional with a solid foundation in data analysis and a growing ability to work independently on analytical projects. This role involves executing data analysis work, building and maintaining dashboards and reports, and collaborating with stakeholders to translate business questions into data-driven insights.



A career with purpose.
A career built on making dreams come true.
A career built on building zero defect homes, cost management, and adherence to schedules.



Role Overview



Mid-level position with growing independence
Executes analytical projects with general direction from senior team members.
Proficient in SQL and Tableau with a drive to deepen technical skills



Your Responsibilities On The Team



Analyze business problems and opportunities, providing data-driven findings and recommendations.
Gather and document analytics and reporting requirements in collaboration with stakeholders.
Contribute to the design, creation, maintenance, and rollout of dashboards, reports, and data tools.
Collaborate with stakeholders across teams to understand data needs and communicate findings clearly.
Perform ad-hoc analyses to find answers to business performance questions.
Prepare and present clear reports and analytical summaries for team and stakeholder review.



Requirements



Strong SQL skills with demonstrated ability to write complex queries independently.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Business Administration, or a related field. (Master’s degree beneficial).
Minimum 3-4 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related role.
Confident with learning new tools, technologies, and methodologies.
Familiarity with data transformation tools such as dbt (a plus, not required).
Proficient in Tableau or similar data visualization tools with hands-on dashboard development experience.
Experience with marketing and sales analytics (media and attribution experience a plus).
Strong communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills.
Curious, detail-oriented, and eager to grow technical and analytical skills over time.



Physical & Office/Site Presence Requirements:

This is primarily a sedentary office position which requires the Data Analyst II to have the ability to operate computer equipment, speak, hear, bend, stoop, reach, lift, and move and carry up to 25 lbs. Finger dexterity is necessary.

This is a position which requires the Data Analyst II frequent walking, standing and climbing stairs in/around homes, models and properties. Must also have the ability to operate computer

equipment, speak, hear, bend, stoop, reach, lift, and move and carry up to 25 lbs. Finger dexterity is necessary.



This information is intended to be a general overview and may be modified by the company due to factors affecting the business.



General Overview of Compensation & Benefits:

We reasonably expect the base compensation offered for this position to range from an annual salary of $95,400.00 - $119,200, subject to adjustment based on business-related factors such as employee qualifications, geographic pay differentials (e.g., cost of labor/living, etc.), and operational considerations.



This position may be eligible for bonuses.
This position may be eligible for commissions.
This position will be eligible for the described benefits listed in the above section in accordance with Company Policy.
This information is intended to be a general overview and may be modified by the Company due to factors affecting the business.



Life at Lennar

At Lennar, we are committed to fostering a supportive and enriching environment for our Associates, offering a comprehensive array of benefits designed to enhance their well-being and professional growth. Our Associates have access to robust health insurance plans, including Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage, ensuring their health needs are well taken care of. Our 401(k) Retirement Plan, complete with a $1 for $1 Company Match up to 5%, helps secure their financial future, while Paid Parental Leave and an Associate Assistance Plan provide essential support during life's critical moments. To further support our Associates, we provide an Education Assistance Program and up to $30,000 in Adoption Assistance, underscoring our commitment to their diverse needs and aspirations. From the moment of hire, they can enjoy up to three weeks of vacation annually, alongside generous Holiday, Sick Leave, and Personal Day policies. Additionally, we offer a New Hire Referral Bonus Program, significant Home Purchase Discounts, and unique opportunities such as the Everyone’s Included Day. At Lennar, we believe in investing in our Associates, empowering them to thrive both personally and professionally. Lennar Associates will have access to these benefits as outlined by Lennar’s policies and applicable plan terms. Visit Lennartotalrewards.com to view our suite of benefits.

Title: Data Analyst (PowerBI, Excel)
Description:
Dice is the leading career destination for tech experts at every stage of their careers. Our client, Ledgent Technology, is seeking the following. Apply via Dice today!

No Corp-to-corp. No 3rd party firms





Position: Data Analyst (Advanced Excel, PowerBI)

Location: 100% onsite in Irvine, CA (Only local candidates will be considered)

Terms: Direct-hire

Compensation: $35.00 to 42.00 per hour (DOE)

Benefits: Annual discretionary bonus + benefits + 3 weeks PTO

Partnered with a client who is hiring an experienced Data Analyst to support the customer experience team by delivering actionable insights that improve customer service performance, on-time delivery, and operational efficiency.

This is a unique, business-facing analytics role where you will combine technical expertise (Power BI, Excel, ERP, CRM systems) with strong business acumen and communication skills to influence decision-making.

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Insights


Analyze customer service metrics (order fulfillment, backlog, on-time delivery, queue performance, expedite requests)
Identify trends and translate findings into actionable recommendations
Ensure data integrity and validate sources


Reporting & Dashboards


Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports
Deliver ad hoc and recurring insights to leadership and cross-functional teams
Continuously improve reporting and identify gaps/opportunities


Operational Support


Monitor backlog and delivery risk; proactively flag issues and recommend mitigation
Partner with customer experience, sales, and operations teams
Understand end-to-end order flow from entry to reporting


Business Contribution


Fulfill reporting requests from leadership
Proactively identify new metrics, trends, and improvement opportunities
Recommend dashboards, enhancements, and process improvements


Required Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Analytics, Business, Finance, Supply Chain, or related field
3+ years in data analytics or similar role
Advanced Power BI and Excel skills
Experience with ERP systems (SAP preferred) and CRM platforms
Strong analytical, data validation, and problem-solving skills
Ability to clearly communicate insights and challenge assumptions

Title: Senior Business Data Analyst, Performance Management
Description:
For over 50 years, LifeLabs has been Canada’s leading provider of laboratory diagnostic information and digital health connectivity systems, enabling patients and healthcare practitioners to diagnose, treat, monitor and prevent disease. We are passionate about empowering healthier Canadians through accessible, accurate, and innovative diagnostic services.

We are committed innovators, operating Canada's first commercial genetics lab, and the country's largest online patient portal, with more than 8 million Canadians receiving their results online. More than 112 million laboratory tests come through LifeLabs’ laboratories annually, and our team of more than 6,000 passionate, caring, and diverse professionals works together as one to provide high quality testing and results that Canadians can trust.

We know that behind every lab requisition, sample being tested, or investment in technology is an individual and their family counting on us. At LifeLabs, you can make a meaningful impact on Canadians’ lives every day.

Our teams are at the heart of everything we do. We are proud to be recognized as one of Canada’s Best Employers, reflecting our deep commitment to our core values of caring, agility, teamwork, and a customer-centered approach. As part of this commitment, LifeLabs prioritizes the ongoing development of our diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) program to better serve the needs of our diverse workforce and the communities we serve. We continue to take steps to challenge ourselves to act with courage and integrity, and to create an environment where people can be their true selves.

These values are not just words on a page, they guide our actions and decisions every day and have come to define our team culture.

Reports to: Senior Manager, Performance Management & Insights

Vacancy status: This posting is for an existing position.

About The Role

We are looking for an experienced and highly skilled Senior Business Data Analyst to join our Performance Management & Insights team. In this role, you will help drive data-informed decision-making across LifeLabs’ national operations by developing trusted reporting, operational KPIs, self-serve Power BI dashboards, and actionable insights that support business performance.

This is not only a reporting role. The successful candidate will help strengthen our operational analytics capability by applying advanced analytics, statistical thinking, forecasting, and automation to identify performance risks, evaluate business outcomes, and support proactive decision-making. You will work closely with operational leaders, Finance, IT, Data Management, and cross-functional partners to translate complex business problems into clear analytical approaches and practical recommendations.

What You Will Do

Reporting, Insight Generation, and Performance Measurement


Develop, maintain, and enhance operational reporting capabilities that provide leaders with timely, accurate, and actionable performance insights.
Design and build comprehensive Power BI reports, self-serve tools, scorecards, and operational health dashboards for Operations leaders, senior leadership, and executive audiences.
Establish, monitor, and evolve critical KPIs that help measure operational performance, business outcomes, productivity, service levels, and the impact of change.
Analyze operational, workforce, financial, and performance data to identify trends, risks, root causes, and opportunities for improvement.
Apply statistical analysis, forecasting, simulation, and basic predictive modelling techniques to support proactive decision-making and operational planning.
Use Python, SQL, Power BI, DAX, M Query, Excel, and AI-enabled tools to automate recurring analysis, improve analytical workflows, and develop scalable insights beyond traditional reporting.
Partner with Finance to support budget optimization, monitor performance against plan, and identify opportunities to reduce variance.
Collaborate with subject matter experts to extract, cleanse, validate, and analyze complex information from multiple data sources.
Support workforce management, capacity planning, and forecasting by using historical data, operational trends, and statistical models to assess staffing needs and service-level risks.


Business Partnership and Collaboration


Partner with Operations leaders to understand business problems, define analytical requirements, and develop insight products that support better decision-making.
Translate complex data and analysis into clear, practical recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Support continuous improvement initiatives by identifying performance gaps, measuring the impact of interventions, and helping leaders prioritize action.
Build a strong understanding of end-to-end business processes across operational departments to ensure analytics are relevant, trusted, and actionable.
Work cross-functionally with Finance, IT, Data Management, Decision Sciences, and operational teams to improve data quality, reporting processes, and analytical capabilities.


Data Governance, Adoption, and Change Management


Support data governance practices to ensure operational reports and dashboards are accurate, consistent, standardized, and aligned to internal definitions.
Validate data sources, business rules, KPIs, and reporting outputs to ensure business users can trust the information being used for decision-making.
Document analytical logic, reporting processes, data definitions, and business rules to support transparency and sustainability.
Lead or support change management activities after dashboard and reporting delivery to help business teams adopt, embed, and fully use data in daily decision-making.
Provide training, guidance, and support to help stakeholders interpret insights and use analytics tools effectively.


What You Bring to the Role


5+ years of experience in business intelligence, data analysis, operational reporting, performance management, or a related analytics role.
University degree in Business, Economics, Statistics, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Engineering, or a related field. A Master’s degree is considered an asset.
Advanced Power BI experience, including dashboard development, data modelling, DAX, Power Query, and building self-serve reporting solutions.
Strong SQL skills, with the ability to extract, join, transform, validate, and analyze data from multiple systems.
Working knowledge of Python for data analysis, automation, exploratory analysis, and statistical modelling using libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Seaborn, or similar tools.
Demonstrated ability to apply statistical concepts such as trend analysis, correlation, regression, variance analysis, hypothesis testing, and confidence intervals to support business decisions.
Experience with forecasting, workforce planning, capacity planning, simulation, or predictive modelling is considered a strong asset.
Familiarity with basic machine learning concepts such as classification, regression, clustering, decision trees, and model evaluation techniques is an asset.
Comfort using AI-enabled tools to improve productivity, accelerate code development, automate repetitive tasks, and enhance analytical workflows.
Strong analytical and conceptual thinking skills, with the ability to move beyond data manipulation to meaningful interpretation and actionable recommendations.
Ability to communicate complex analysis clearly to non-technical audiences through reports, dashboards, presentations, and executive-level storytelling.
Strong attention to detail, with a focus on data quality, validation, documentation, and governance.
Demonstrated ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and operate in a fast-paced environment.
Strong collaboration skills and the ability to work with stakeholders at multiple levels across the organization.
Financial acumen and experience conducting financial or operational performance analysis is an asset.
Experience in healthcare, regulated environments, workforce management, or large-scale operations is an asset.


The Ideal Candidate

The ideal candidate is a technically strong business analyst who can combine Power BI expertise, SQL, Python, statistical thinking, and business judgment to solve operational problems. You are comfortable building dashboards, but you are equally comfortable asking what decision the dashboard is meant to support. You can work with imperfect data, validate assumptions, explain trade-offs, and turn complex analysis into clear recommendations for leaders.

You are curious, detail-oriented, and proactive. You use modern analytics and AI-enabled tools to work smarter, automate repetitive tasks, and develop scalable solutions. You are motivated by helping the business make better, faster, and more confident decisions.

Why Join Us?

At LifeLabs, you’ll join a collaborative, data-driven environment where your expertise in analytics and performance measurement directly supports national operations. As part of the Performance Management & Insights team, you will go beyond reporting to build Power BI dashboards, operational KPIs, and self-serve analytics that drive decision-making across the organization. You’ll apply SQL, Python, statistical methods, and automation tools to uncover trends, evaluate performance, and support forecasting, workforce planning, and continuous improvement. This role offers the opportunity to turn complex data into actionable insights, partnering closely with Operations, Finance, IT, and Data teams to improve performance and enable better, faster decisions in a fast-paced healthcare environment.

What We Offer: Salary range: $81,431– 103,825 CAD annually (commensurate with experience)

LifeLabs’ compensation programs are commensurate based on the role, skill, effort, responsibility and working conditions, irrespective of gender, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age or any other personal characteristics. Pay programs are communicated regularly in an accessible and transparent manner.

LifeLabs is also proud to offer resources, opportunities, as well as a collaborative and supportive environment that enables our team members to thrive.

In Addition To a Competitive Compensation Package, LifeLabs Provides a Comprehensive Total Rewards Program, Specific To The Job Position. Your Package May Include


Employee Group Benefits: Competitive coverage for employees and their families to support their overall health and wellness needs, including Extended Health Care, Dental Care, and Life Insurance.
Retirement Savings Plan
Vacation and Wellness Days
Employee Wellness and Giving Programs: Our award winning mental, physical and financial wellness programs aim to address the comprehensive well-being of our team members, including resources like the Employee & Family Assistance Program, financial planning tools, and employee recognition initiatives.
Professional development and membership reimbursement, access to preferred rates and discount programs, including WorkPerks, Home and Auto Insurance, Costco Membership, etc., and optional health-related benefits.


In accordance with LifeLabs’ Accessibility Policy, and the applicable Accessibility Acts within the provinces we operate in, accommodations are available by request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the recruitment and selection process. For a confidential inquiry or to request an accommodation, please contact your recruiter or email careers@lifelabs.com.

Vaccinations are highly encouraged at LifeLabs’. Vaccinations and/or immunization screening may be mandatory for selected employees if regulated by provincial or regional governments, or through employer-led vaccination policies in the facilities we service. Please ensure you ask if this position requires the successful candidate to be vaccinated or undergo immunization screening.

Ready to empower healthier Canadians? Apply today!

Title: Performance Analyst
Description:
About the Role

We’re seeking a Data Analyst to support business growth by delivering insightful, actionable analysis. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with complex datasets, uncovering trends, and influencing decisions through data-driven recommendations.

You’ll partner closely with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, improve performance, and support strategic initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Analyze complex datasets to identify trends, insights, and root causes of business performance
Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations for stakeholders
Build and maintain reports, dashboards, and analytical tools to support decision-making
Monitor key performance metrics and provide regular, timely insights
Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across reporting and models
Develop predictive models and analytical frameworks to support business strategy
Assess customer behavior trends and recommend strategies to drive growth and profitability
Identify areas of underperformance and partner with teams to implement improvements
Support marketing, sales, and operational initiatives through data analysis and reporting

Qualifications

Degree in Finance, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field
1–3 years of experience in a data or analytics-focused role
Strong proficiency in SQL and Excel; experience with Python, R, or SAS is an asset
Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail
Ability to clearly communicate insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Self-starter with the ability to manage priorities and meet deadlines independently

Nice to Have

Experience working with large datasets or in a fast-paced, data-driven environment
Exposure to predictive analytics or statistical modeling
Experience supporting marketing, sales, or revenue-focused teams

Why Apply?

Opportunity to work on high-impact, data-driven initiatives
Collaborative and supportive team environment
Strong potential for growth and skill development
Flexible work arrangements

Title: Sr. BI Commercial Data Analyst
Description:
Job Brief

The Sr. BI Commercial Data Analyst plays a key role in developing, maintaining, and innovating data analytics reporting solutions within the commercial analytics team. This position will design, develop, and manage Power BI reports and dashboards.

At DHL, our people are our greatest asset! Everyone’s contribution drives us to be the world's #1 logistics company.

Certified as a Great Place to Work and as a Top Employer, we're dedicated to fostering a positive, collaborative, and supportive environment for all. Our commitment and engagement with Our People ensure we continuously build a workplace we're all proud of. Plus, with competitive compensation and exceptional perks, we make sure your personal life shines just as brightly as your career.

Summary

The Sr. BI Commercial Data Analyst plays a key role in developing, maintaining, and innovating data analytics reporting solutions within the commercial analytics team. This position will design, develop, and manage Power BI reports and dashboards by leveraging data analytics skills complemented with expertise in Power BI and a clear understanding of Azure Data Lake. This role will also be responsible for conducting piece level data (PLD) analysis to create customer profiles, working closely with the sales team to understand requirements and delivering accurate, logically sound analytical solutions. This position will support continuous improvement of existing reporting and analytical processes while contributing to broader data analytics projects as required.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities


Design, develop, and publish Power BI reports and templates that are visually intuitive and aligned with end-user needs and business requirements
Ensure all commercial Power BI reports are logically sound, mathematically accurate, and free of errors prior to deployment
Gather and understand business requirements from stakeholders and translate them into logical, accurate, and user-friendly Power BI reports and dashboards
Maintain and monitor all published Power BI reports to ensure minimal downtime and that report logic remains aligned with evolving business requirements
Conduct PLD analyses by working closely with the sales team to understand requirements, develop comprehensive solutions, and provide follow-up support to ensure findings are clearly understood
Ensure all PLD analytical solutions are logically and mathematically sound, free of errors, and meet the requirements of the requesting stakeholder
Proactively identify opportunities to make PLD analyses more comprehensive, insightful, and efficient over time
Model and optimize logistics process to support product management via Power BI, Excel, Access and Python or other statistical tools
Understand and become subject matter expert on USPS requirements and postal regulation on logistics questions (e.g. product classification, zone pricing, dangerous good, among others.)
Support ad-hoc requests from sales and leadership team by providing data driven conclusion and recommendation to help drive product strategy
Leverage Azure Data Lake to extract, transform, and analyze large volumes of data to support reporting and analytical needs
Support additional data analytics projects across the commercial team as and when required
Work both independently and collaboratively within the team to deliver high quality analytical outputs within agreed timelines
Perform other duties and special projects as assigned


Education & Experience


Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Business Analytics, Computer Science or a related quantitative field required
Master’s degree/MBA in Business, Operations, Business Logistics Engineering or a related field preferred.
5 years of related work experience in data analytics, product management, management consulting or data science; 2 to 3 years working knowledge of Microsoft Power BI with good understanding of Azure Data Lake and Data Bricks


Requirements & Skills


Strong analytical, problem solving and quantitative analysis skills
Knowledge expert of Microsoft Power BI
Knowledge of databases, data analytics and visualization tools (Oracle SQL Developer, Python, Power BI and Tableau)
Proficient in Microsoft Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS), Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory, and Azure Synapse Analytics for data extraction, transformation, and analysis
Strong working knowledge of SQL, PySpark/Python, DAX, and Power Query for data processing and Power BI report development
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Strong planning, project management and organizational skills
Proficient in Excel, PowerPoint, MS Access, Visio
High attention to detail & forward thinking
Ability to work individually or within a team in a fast-paced environment
Ability to lead cross-functional teams and indirectly manage more junior team members


Physical Demands

Business travel as required (
Regular sitting at workstation for 25 – 75% of the work shift
Frequent standing and walking


As a leading logistics company in one of the fastest growing industries, at DHL eCommerce, we offer our employees, and their dependents benefits and incentives to make them successful at work and home.


Competitive Pay
Bonus Programs
Retirement Savings - 401k with company match
Medical, Dental, Vision, Well-being programs
FSA/HSA availability
Tuition Reimbursement
Paid Time Off including vacation and sick time
Company Paid Holidays and Floating Holidays
Paid Parental Leave
Employee Discount Program
Employee Assistance & Work Life Program
Short Term and Long-Term Disability
Life Insurance

  • Support additional data analytics projects across the commercial team as and when required
  • Work both independently and collaboratively within the team to deliver high quality analytical outputs within agreed timelines
  • Perform other duties and special projects as assigned

Education & Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Business Analytics, Computer Science or a related quantitative field required
  • Master’s degree/MBA in Business, Operations, Business Logistics Engineering or a related field preferred.
  • 5 years of related work experience in data analytics, product management, management consulting or data science; 2 to 3 years working knowledge of Microsoft Power BI with good understanding of Azure Data Lake and Data Bricks

Requirements & Skills

  • Strong analytical, problem solving and quantitative analysis skills
  • Knowledge expert of Microsoft Power BI
  • Knowledge of databases, data analytics and visualization tools (Oracle SQL Developer, Python, Power BI and Tableau)
  • Proficient in Microsoft Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS), Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory, and Azure Synapse Analytics for data extraction, transformation, and analysis
  • Strong working knowledge of SQL, PySpark/Python, DAX, and Power Query for data processing and Power BI report development
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Strong planning, project management and organizational skills
  • Proficient in Excel, PowerPoint, MS Access, Visio
  • High attention to detail & forward thinking
  • Ability to work individually or within a team in a fast-paced environment
  • Ability to lead cross-functional teams and indirectly manage more junior team members

Title: Data Analyst, Clinical Analytics and Reporting
Description:
Posting Date

04/28/2026

2000 16th St, Denver, Colorado, 80202-5117, United States of America

Clinical Data Analyst, Clinical Analytics & Reporting Team

The Clinical Analytics & Reporting Team is responsible for designing, developing and overseeing analytical solutions for our clinical and operations executives in support of DaVita’s clinical strategy. Our team’s mission is to improve patient quality of life in terms of care safety, quality, and efficiency by transforming data into actionable information that guides strategic decisions, supports clinical innovation, and informs operating tactics. This position will primarily support the Home National Team.

The Clinical Data Analyst’s primary objective is to partner with diverse teams from around the enterprise to produce analytical insights that support analytical products across a broad range of clinical initiatives.

A successful teammate exemplifies personal behaviors in line with DaVita’s core values: service excellence, integrity, team, continuous improvement, accountability, fulfillment, and fun.

Sound like you? Then you might be a great fit for the role of Clinical Data Analyst on DaVita’s Clinical Analytics & Reporting team.

Responsibilities


Create, maintain, and distribute reports related to clinical outcomes, surveillance, and risk mitigation on a regular basis
Utilize multiple computational languages, platforms and environments to mine and manage data in preparation for analysis and/or reporting
Provide clinical insights and data trends to support and inform a multitude of projects and initiatives across the organization
Present data to and work closely with a variety of stakeholders including senior-level operators and teammates in the clinics
Synthesize results in a non-technical manner to inform recommendations and next steps for leadership
Create cohesive presentations based on findings, summarizing insights from analysis
Act as a collaborative partner during strategy meetings
Become an subject matter expert and thought partner in clinical and methodologic areas
Willing to travel (up to 10% of time)


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree required; quantitative fields such as biostatistics, statistics, informatics, computer science, or math strongly recommended
1-2 years of prior relevant work experience preferred
Demonstrated ability to learn and develop proficiency in any or all of the following as needed: SQL, Tableau, Anaplan, Python, R, etc.
Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint proficiency required
Advanced critical thinking, strong work ethic, creative mindset, adept communication skills, attention to detail, design-mindedness, flexibility with change, comfort with ambiguity, efficient time management, technical aptitude, and business acumen


Language Skills

Fluent in the written and verbal skills necessary to perform successfully the essential functions, duties and responsibilities of the position

Vision Requirements

Vision adequate to perform essential duties and responsibilities of position

Physical Demands

Physical requirements can vary. These must be reviewed with management. However, in general, the position requires the following physical activities:


Ability to lift minimum of 2 pounds to a maximum of 15 pounds unassisted; ability to sit for extended periods of time with some standing, stooping, walking, stretching, reaching, lifting; moderate range of body motions
Ability to use computer and all peripherals for extended periods of time


What We’ll Provide

More than just pay, our DaVita Rewards package connects teammates to what matters most. Teammates are eligible to begin receiving benefits on the first day of the month following or coinciding with one month of continuous employment. Below are some of our benefit offerings.


Comprehensive benefits: Medical, dental, vision, 401(k) match, paid time off, PTO cash out
Support for you and your family: Family resources, EAP counseling sessions, access Headspace®, backup child and elder care, maternity/paternity leave and more
Professional development programs: DaVita offers a variety of programs to help strong performers grow within their career and also offers on-demand virtual leadership and development courses through DaVita’s online training platform StarLearning.


At DaVita, we strive to be a community first and a company second. We want all teammates to experience DaVita as "a place where I belong." Our goal is to embed belonging into everything we do in our Village, so that it becomes part of who we are. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and comply with state and federal affirmative action requirements. Individuals are recruited, hired, assigned and promoted without regard to race, national origin, religion, age, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, protected veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.

This position will be open for a minimum of three days.

Title: Data Analyst II - UHC COVE
Description:
At UnitedHealthcare, we're simplifying the health care experience, creating healthier communities and removing barriers to quality care. The work you do here impacts the lives of millions of people for the better. Come build the health care system of tomorrow, making it more responsive, affordable and optimized. Ready to make a difference? Join us to start Caring. Connecting. Growing together

UnitedHealthcare is seeking a highly skilled Data Analyst II to join our Consumer Operations Vital and Experience Hub (COVE). COVE monitors member experience, provider experience, and core operational experiences, with a focus on early detection and resolution of issues and data driven decision making across multiple lines of business.

As a Data Analyst II, you will be responsible for building and refining tools to analyze complex operational data, generate actionable insights, and contribute to advanced analytics solutions, including AI/ML enabled use cases, in support of multiple lines of business and shared services organizations.

This position is hybrid, with a minimum of 4 days per week in office at the 9700 Building in Minnetonka, MN.

Primary Responsibilities


Support the full analytics and data solution lifecycle, including research, proof of concepts, design, development, testing, deployment, and ongoing maintenance
Analyze operational and experience data to identify opportunities for process improvement, cost optimization, and experience enhancement across consumer, provider, and core operations
Work with diverse data sources including NPS trends, consumer and provider verbatims, digital portal interactions, and critical operational metrics
Collaborate with cross functional partners to define business problems and develop analytic solutions
Perform as an individual contributor by querying and integrating data from multiple sources, wrangling datasets, and building dashboards and analyses that surface insights and opportunities
Apply data storytelling techniques to clearly communicate findings to technical and non technical audiences
Anticipate stakeholder needs by engaging in creative problem solving and ideation to bring new, relevant analytic content to Operations
Contribute to team and organizational growth through knowledge sharing, collaboration, mentorship, and continuous self development


You'll be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role as well as provide development for other roles you may be interested in.

Required Qualifications


3+ years of experience in a highly quantitative field such as analytics, statistics, or computer science
2+ years of hands on experience in data analysis, data driven investigations, data visualization, and insight generation for operational or leadership audiences
Advanced proficiency in SQL
Advanced experience building analytics solutions using business intelligence tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or equivalent
Intermediate to advanced experience working with large scale datasets, including terabyte level data
Demonstrated experience performing statistical analysis and presenting results to non technical audiences
Experience extracting and integrating data from Enterprise Data Warehouses and Enterprise Data Lakes
Experience with cloud based analytic services such as Snowflake or DataBricks
Advanced proficiency in Excel and Microsoft Office tools
Must live in the state of Minnesota and be able to work in the Minnetonka, MN office 4 days a week & 1 remote day


Preferred Qualifications


Proficiency with Python or other analytical programming languages
Solid theoretical and practical knowledge of machine learning or artificial intelligence algorithms and techniques applied to analytics use cases
Experience creating and delivering presentations to varying levels of leadership
Healthcare or health insurance industry experience
Solid problem solving skills with the ability to identify root causes and recommend data supported improvements
Proven ability to work effectively in a fast paced environment while managing multiple priorities


Pay is based on several factors including but not limited to local labor markets, education, work experience, certifications, etc. In addition to your salary, we offer benefits such as, a comprehensive benefits package, incentive and recognition programs, equity stock purchase and 401k contribution (all benefits are subject to eligibility requirements). No matter where or when you begin a career with us, you'll find a far-reaching choice of benefits and incentives. The salary for this role will range from $72,800 to $130,000 annually based on full-time employment. We comply with all minimum wage laws as applicable.

At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes - an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.

UnitedHealth Group is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer under applicable law and qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, national origin, religion, age, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, or protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by local, state, or federal laws, rules, or regulations.

UnitedHealth Group is a drug-free workplace. Candidates are required to pass a drug test before beginning employment.

Title: Enterprise Data Analyst
Description:
Since 2012, we've grown to become one of the leading single-family rental companies and homebuilders in the country, recently recognized as a top employer by Fortune and Great Place To Work®.  At AMH, our goal is to simplify the experience of leasing a home through professional management and maintenance support, so our residents can focus on what really matters to them, wherever they are in life. 

The Enterprise Data Analyst is responsible for analyzing and interpreting collected data, identifying trends, creating reports, dashboards, scorecards, and tailored datasets for self-service reporting. Collaborates with internal stakeholders to provide recommendations by leveraging data modeling to support informed business decisions. Works cross-functionally with internal teams (e.g., finance and engineering) to support data, reporting needs, identify and address data gaps. Leverages company AI tools to model and forecast data to establish consistent business language definitions and metrics.

Responsibilities


Develops analytical content to include reports, dashboards, semantic models, and datasets by utilizing business intelligence tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, SQL, Databricks, etc.). Supports end-user adoption of reporting through documentation and guidance.
Writes SQL-based solutions and leverages AI tools to support the company’s reporting requirements by utilizing the company data platform.
Collaborates with the Finance team to conduct data analysis, fulfill data requests, and provide insights on company performance to enhance and refine processes and deliverables. Creates new features into existing report packages to improve deliverables.
Ensures data accuracy by identifying issues, correcting metric definitions, and validating results before reporting


Requirements


High school diploma or GED required.
Bachelor’s degree in mathematics, finance, accounting, economics, statistics, business management, business analytics, data science and/or a related field preferred.
Minimum three (3) years of experience working in business intelligence, analytics, financial reporting, and/or a related field required.
Minimum three (3) years of experience in requirements gathering and business analysis techniques required, including a strong understanding of the business intelligence (BI) development life cycle, conducting stakeholder interviews, and translating business needs into BI solutions.
Experience in financial modeling preferred.
Intermediate experience querying relational databases and data warehouses (e.g., SQL Server, Databricks, Snowflake, etc.) required.
Intermediate Microsoft Excel experience required, including writing and using advanced functions and formulas (e.g., lookup and reference, nested logical expressions, aggregation, etc.) as well as advanced Excel features (e.g., conditional formatting, data validation, pivot tables, external data connections).
Working knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel, Outlook, Word) required.
Working knowledge of Tableau or Power BI is highly preferred.
Excellent verbal and written communication and problem-solving skills.
Strong business analysis skills and technical aptitude.
Strong understanding of finance concepts.


Compensation

The anticipated pay range/scale for this position is $91,597.00 to $114,496.00 Annually. Actual starting base pay within this range will depend on factors including geographic location, education, training, skills, and relevant experience.

Additional Compensation

This position is eligible to receive a discretionary annual bonus.

Perks and Benefits

Employees also have the opportunity to participate in medical, dental and vision insurance; flexible spending accounts and/or health savings accounts; dependent savings accounts; 401(k) with company matching contributions; employee stock purchase plan; and a tuition reimbursement program. The Company provides 9 paid holidays per year, and, upon hire, new employees will accrue paid time off (PTO) at a rate of 0.0577 hours of PTO per hour worked, up to a maximum of 120 hours per year.

Background Check

Background check required. AMH will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records in accordance with all applicable federal, state and local laws, which may include the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act.

CA Privacy Notice: To learn more about what information we collect when you apply for a job, and how we use that information, please see our CA Job Applicant Privacy Notice found at https://www.amh.com/ca-privacy-notice.

The Company provides 9 paid holidays per year, and, upon hire, new employees will accrue paid time off (PTO) at a rate of 0.0577 hours of PTO per hour worked, up to a maximum of 120 hours per year.

Background Check

Background check required. AMH will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records in accordance with all applicable federal, state and local laws, which may include the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act.

CA Privacy Notice: To learn more about what information we collect when you apply for a job, and how we use that information, please see our CA Job Applicant Privacy Notice found at https://www.amh.com/ca-privacy-notice.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
An exciting career awaits you

At MPC, we’re committed to being a great place to work – one that welcomes new ideas, encourages diverse perspectives, develops our people, and fosters a collaborative team environment.

Position Summary

The Central Commercial Management Data & Insights team’s mission is to provide exceptional, cutting-edge analytics and meaningful insights to empower our Commercial organization to thrive and achieve unparalleled success. We do that by enhancing the trust and confidence in the data and analytics within Commercial, reducing barriers to timely, actionable insights.

This position belongs to a family of jobs with increasing responsibility, competency, and skill level. Actual position title and pay grade will be based on the selected candidate’s experience and qualifications.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:


Acquires, cleans and preprocesses raw data from various sources to ensure accuracy and reliability; maintains knowledge of key systems and queries those systems for data.
Conducts in-depth analysis of large datasets to extract meaningful insights and trends
Develops and implements analytical models to forecast future trends and outcomes
Develops and performs statistical analysis to identify patterns and correlations in the data
Creates visually compelling and easy-to-understand dashboards and reports
Communicates findings to stakeholders through charts, graphs, and presentations
Ensures integrity and quality of data through regular audits and validation processes
Works closely with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and deliver insights
Drive continuous improvement and defect elimination in organization using data insights
Establishes and supports data governance to ensure data is fit for optimum use by the organization
Partners and leads initiatives to build sustainable data pipelines, automations, and advanced analytics solutions

Bachelor's Degree
2 years experience
